data.title: Kohl’s Stock (KSS) Goes Ballistic on Earnings Beat and Stronger Outlook
data.description:
Shares of Kohl’s (KSS) exploded sharply on Thursday after the U.S. retailer revealed its quarterly earnings beat Wall Street forecasts despite low sales. The management also said sales momentum is starting to improve as the firm works to steady operations and win back its market share.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Kohl’s Stock Fires Up on Earnings Beat and Improving Sales

The price of KSS climbed more than 17% in a single day after the company reported solid earnings. The stock is now trading above $15, reflecting a 30.8% increase over the past five days, according to TipRanks data. 

The massive price surge comes after Khol’s reported its best sales performance in four years during Q1 2026, helping lift investor confidence in its business. The retailer said its comparable sales declined 1.1%, a major improvement from the 2.8% drop seen in Q1 2025. At the same time, the company’s net sales decreased by 1.7%.  

Kohl’s recorded a quarterly revenue of $3 billion, slightly above Wall Street’s $2.99 billion forecast. The retailer also reported a quarterly loss of 13 cents per share, beating analyst forecasts for a 19-cent loss.

Meanwhile, CEO Michael Bender said the company is “knocking on the door of growth” as it continues to cut expenses. It is also improving its inventory management practices and boosting its balance sheet ahead of the next quarter.

Kohl’s Maintains Full-Year Outlook Despite Economic Pressures

Despite struggling with ongoing low sales, Kohl’s is maintaining its full-year outlook. The retailer expects net sales and comparable sales to range from a 2% decline to flat growth for the year, while adjusted earnings are expected to range from $1 to $1.60 per share.

The company claimed its sales are under pressure due to inflation and high energy prices. As a result, lower- and middle-income consumers, who are the largest part of Kohl’s customer base, are spending less.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Cisco (CSCO) Has Rallied Hard. I Still Like the AI Story

I remain bullish on CSCO because of accelerating AI demand and strong cash generation.

Also, IBM continues to trade at a reasonable valuation, while its core business and underlying dividend bolster the investment case’s margin of safety. Thus, I am bullish on IBM stock.

data.title: PayPal (PYPL) Makes History With First-Ever Sri Lankan Banking Partner
data.description:
PayPal (PYPL) and Sampath Bank have announced a new partnership to expand global payment access for users in Sri Lanka. Through this deal, Sampath Bank will become the first to enable PayPal account linking and withdrawals. The partnership is designed to make international payments easier and more accessible for Sri Lankans.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Sampath Bank Expands Global Payments Through PayPal Integration

PayPal’s system has been integrated into Sampath Vishwa, an online payment portal offered by Sampath Bank. The new alliance marks a major step in Sri Lanka’s payments space, with Sampath becoming the first bank in the nation to offer this service. 

The partnership is set to expand payment access for users and businesses across the country. Currently, demand for safe international payment systems continues to rise as freelancers, online businesses, exporters, and remote workers expand their presence in global markets. As such, many users need better ways to access cross-border payments and receive foreign income. 

With PayPal now part of Sri Lanka’s online banking system, users can finally receive and transfer money through their local bank accounts. The deal will also help people work more easily in the global online trade market. Furthermore, it supports foreign currency inflows into Sri Lanka as more users engage in remote work and online businesses.

How Sri Lankans Can Access PayPal Via Sampath

The new deal automatically links PayPal to the Sampath Vishwa mobile app, allowing users to connect their accounts and manage foreign payments more easily. Funds can be withdrawn directly into LKR or USD accounts, depending on the user’s choice. Those who do not have an existing PayPal account can also set one up in the app, making it easier to get started. 

The service is designed for freelancers, SMEs, exporters, and online sellers who rely on global payment flows. It makes payments easier and helps improve Sri Lanka’s online banking by adding more ways to send and receive foreign money. This, in turn, helps the bank stand out more as it grows its digital financial services.

Sanjaya Gunawardana, Managing Director and CEO of Sampath Bank, described the new deal with PayPal as a step toward opening up global opportunities for Sri Lankans. He added that people should not be held back from taking part in the global economy just because of access limitations.

Is PayPal Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

According to TipRanks analysts, PayPal (PYPL) is rated a Hold. The stock has a 12-month projected price target of $48.71, implying an 11% upside. Out of 28 Wall Street analysts, 3 rate it a Buy, 22 a Hold, and 3 a Sell. Investors can track PYPL’s latest ratings and price targets via the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Cerebras (CBRS) vs Nvidia (NVDA) — Which AI Stock Is a Better Buy Now?
data.description:
Cerebras Systems (CBRS) had a mega public market debut, with its stock rising sharply on its first day of trading. This shows strong investor interest in new artificial intelligence (AI) firms entering the stock market. It also highlights growing attention to new public players in the chipmaker space as the sector expands quickly.

Meet Samuel – Your Personal Investing Prophet

Two ETFs to trade CBRS long or short: CBRX & CBRZ

At the same time, Nvidia (NVDA) remains the leading company in AI chips, although newer firms like Cerebras are seeing major demand. This creates a clearer picture of rising competition in the AI chip space, as investors look at both established leaders.

Cerebras Gains Early Attention With Strong IPO Debut

Cerebras Systems publicly launched on May 14, 2026, and its stock rose 68% on its first day of trading. The strong debut shows heavy investor interest in new AI chip companies entering public markets.

The company uses a wafer-scale chip design that treats an entire silicon wafer as one unit. This approach focuses on boosting processing capacity and improving performance for large AI workloads. 

Cerebras is still in an early growth stage, but investor attention is rising as demand for AI infrastructure continues to expand. The company is also positioning itself as a new public entrant in the fast-growing, competitive AI chip space.

Nvidia Maintains Leadership in AI Chip Demand

Nvidia still leads the AI chip market by a landslide, controlling about 86% of the AI data center space. Strong demand from major tech firms continues to support its top position.

Its systems are also designed to operate flexibly, allowing smaller chips to be grouped together for different tasks. This makes Nvidia’s technology widely usable for both large and small companies. 

Another key strength of Nvidia is CUDA, its long-standing software ecosystem that supports a large developer community. This system makes it difficult for companies to shift demand away from Nvidia, allowing the firm to maintain its lead over other AI chip rivals.

Cerebras vs Nvidia: Which Stock Looks Stronger Right Now

Cerebras offers early-stage growth potential, supported by strong IPO interest and a different chip design approach. However, it is still building long-term market strength, with the CBRS stock currently trading at $256.78 per share after its public debut.

Nvidia, on the other hand, already holds a dominant position, a large ecosystem, and wide industry adoption. Its leadership is supported by its size and well-established tech infrastructure. Presently, NVDA is trading at $215.53 per share. Even though Cerebras trades at a higher price, NVDA has a proven track record and broader market adoption.

Based on current position, stability, and adoption, NVDa is rated a Strong Buy by Wall Street analysts tracked by TipRanks. However, given CBRS’s early entry into the market, its growth outlook is still closely watched. 

Is Nvidia Stock a Good Buy Now?

According to TipRanks’ analysts, Nvidia (NVDA) is rated a Strong Buy, with an average price target of $301, showing a 49% upside. This suggests analysts still expect strong growth potential, even as competition in AI chips intensifies. Investors can track NVDA’s latest ratings and price targets via the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Is Losing the AI Coding War Thanks to GitHub’s Unending Outages
data.description:
GitHub, Microsoft’s (MSFT) developer platform, is facing a growing reliability crisis as demand for artificial intelligence (AI) coding pushes its systems to the limit. This demand has caused the platform to suffer multiple outages over the past few years, frustrating many users. Now, as Microsoft slowly loses its ground, competition from newer AI coding platforms is challenging the tech giant’s early lead in the sector. 

Meet Samuel – Your Personal Investing Prophet

Explore AMZO for 2X short leverage on AMZN

GitHub Outages Strain Systems and Raise User Concerns

Between May 2025 and April 2026, GitHub recorded 257 separate incidents, 48 of which were major outages. Since March this year, the platform has experienced more than a dozen outages, lasting over an hour. 

The surge in AI-assisted coding has been the primary driver behind these challenges. As more developers adopted AI tools to write and review code, the load on GitHub grew faster than the platform could handle, exposing weaknesses in the system. 

A major factor behind the instability is GitHub’s slow and incomplete migration to Azure, Microsoft’s cloud computing platform. Currently, only part of GitHub’s traffic has been moved to Azure data centers, while its older systems are still under heavy workload. This split setup has struggled under the extra workload, contributing to GitHub’s recurring downtime.

Developers and businesses have also voiced their frustration about the issue. Some warned that GitHub is becoming unreliable for serious production work. In addition to outages, the platform has faced security incidents and leadership changes after the exit of its former CEO. Together, these problems have added more pressure on its engineering teams and raised questions about GitHub’s stability. 

Microsoft Faces Rising Competition in AI Coding Space 

Microsoft’s GitHub Copilot has been leading the AI-assisted coding sector for years, but the competition has shifted rapidly recently. Tools like Cursor, built by Anysphere, a software firm, and Claude Code, made by Anthropic, an AI company, are now catching up, as more developers and businesses use them.

According to reports, Cursor has already overtaken Copilot in usage among some developer groups. At the same time, other coding platforms from firms like ChatGPT creator OpenAI, GitLab (GTLB), Amazon (AMZN), and Atlassian (TEAM) are also gaining investors’ interest. These rivals benefit from faster development cycles and stronger adoption of AI-native coding workflows.

Despite the heated competition, GitHub remains one of the largest developer platforms globally, with over 180 million users and a strong base in AI coding. However, increasing preference for newer AI tools is putting pressure on Microsoft to strengthen its Copilot’s capabilities.

Should I Buy Microsoft Stock Now?

Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks rate Microsoft (MSFT) a Strong Buy. The stock currently trades around $419, with a 12-month projected average price target of $559.98 and 33.80% upside potential. Out of 35 analysts, 33 rate it a Buy, 2 a Hold, and 0 a Sell. For more information on the stock’s performance, price target, and rating, visit the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

Still, there was some good news, with the Pro segment continuing to show resilience and the company gaining market share in a difficult environment.

data.title: Dividend Aristocrat Chubb (CB) Approves 33rd Annual Payout Increase and $7.5B Share Repurchase Program
data.description:
Dividend Aristocrat Chubb (CB) has extended its long record of returning cash to shareholders, approving its 33rd straight annual dividend increase. The insurer raised its annual payout by 5.2% to $4.08 per share and introduced a new $7.5 billion share repurchase program following its 2026 annual general meeting.

Meet Samuel – Your Personal Investing Prophet

  • Start a conversation with TipRanks’ trusted, data-backed investment intelligence
  • Ask Samuel about stocks, your portfolio, or the market and get instant, personalized insights in seconds

The quarterly dividend will rise to $1.02 per share. It is payable on July 2, 2026, to shareholders on record as of June 12, 2026.

The new buyback plan, which becomes effective July 1, will replace the current program. It gives Chubb flexibility to deploy excess capital. While the company did not outline a specific timeline for repurchases, the authorization signals confidence in its long‑term cash generation and ability to navigate a complex insurance market.

Chubb’s strong underwriting, global scale, solid cash flow, and disciplined capital management have long made it a favorite among income‑focused investors. The latest dividend hike and fresh buyback reflect stability, even as the broader financial sector faces macro pressure and rising claims costs.

With a long history of steady dividend growth and a new repurchase plan in place, Chubb remains one of the most dependable income names in the market.

Is CB Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, CB stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on seven Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the last three months. At $344.59, the average Chubb stock price target implies a 4.34% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Nvidia Increased Its Dividend 2,400%. Why Some Investors Say It’s Not Enough
data.description:
Chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has increased its dividend by an astounding 2,400%. But that hasn’t been enough to satisfy some investors.

Meet Samuel – Your Personal Investing Prophet

Explore NVDS for 2X short leverage on NVDA

The maker of microchips and processors announced that it is increasing its quarterly dividend to $0.25 per share from $0.01 previously. Additionally, Nvidia announced a new $80 billion stock buyback program as it focuses on returning value to shareholders.

However, while the dividend increase is massive in percentage terms, it remains a pittance to many investors who have taken to social media to complain about the increase. Critics note that Nvidia’s dividend yield currently stands at a tiny 0.018% and say that a distribution of $0.25 per share is small potatoes considering Nvidia just reported a profit of $58.3 billion, up 211% from a year earlier.

High Expectations for Nvidia

Critics also grouse that Nvidia should be more generous with stockholders given that its first-quarter revenue of $81.62 billion was up 85% from a year earlier, and that the company now has a market valuation of $5.33 trillion, making it the world’s largest publicly traded concern.

Management also boasted that revenue in Nvidia’s AI data center unit doubled from a year ago to $75.2 billion in the latest quarter, and forecast $91 billion in sales for the current quarter. Disgruntled shareholders say this proves that Nvidia can afford to pay an even higher quarterly dividend.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has acknowledged the calls for greater shareholder rewards, but has said that the company needs to reinvest its profits to drive its continued growth and meet global demand for its microchips and semiconductors that power AI workloads. Nvidia spent $6.04 billion on capital expenditures over the last 12 months.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia’s stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 40 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $281.97 implies 26% upside from current levels. These ratings could change after the company’s financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Costco Stock (COST) Hits 52-Week High as Company Raises Dividend 13%
data.description:
The stock of Costco Wholesale (COST) is at a 52-week high after the grocery retailer raised its quarterly dividend by 13%.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

COST stock traded as high as $1,070.67 per share on May 18, bringing its year-to-date gain to 24%. The shares got a boost after Costco announced a 13% increase to its quarterly dividend, taking it to $1.47 per share. Going forward, Costco will pay an annualized dividend of $5.88 a share.

The increased dividend should take the yield on COST stock above 0.60%. The warehouse club has not hiked its distribution to shareholders for 22 consecutive years. Costco is close to becoming a Dividend Aristocrat, defined as a company that has increased its dividend payment for at least 25 consecutive years.

A Special Dividend?

While investors are cheering the latest rise in Costco’s base dividend, many are still holding out for a one-time special dividend to be issued by the company. Costco has a history of paying hefty special dividends to shareholders every few years.

Costco typically issues a large special dividend every two to three years when excess cash builds up on its balance sheet. The last special dividend of $15 a share was announced in December 2023 and paid in early 2024. Analysts and investors say the company is due to issue another special dividend.

Is COST Stock a Buy?

Costco’s stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 22 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 15 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average COST price target of $1,098.44 implies 3% upside from current levels.

data.title: 7%-Plus Dividend Yield and Double-Digit Upside: Raymond James Picks 2 Dividend Stocks to Buy
data.description:
The first-quarter earnings are mainly behind us, and a few things are clear. To start with, it’s been a strong earnings season – the strongest in the past five quarters. Earnings are showing year-over-year growth well in excess of 25%, and earnings revisions are showing the fastest-paced increase in four years. Multiple factors are supporting earnings this season, including solid consumer sentiment and continued strength in the tech mega-caps.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Watching the situation from Raymond James, CIO Larry Adam tags margins as a key point, writing, “The resilience of US corporate profitability has been striking. Despite tariff pressures and higher energy and input costs, S&P 500 margins rose for a fifth consecutive quarter, reaching a record high of 16%. To put that in perspective, margins have increased by three percentage points over the past two years, an outcome that is historically unprecedented outside of recessionary recovery periods. That said, early signs of pressure are beginning to emerge. While the Iran conflict affected only one month of 1Q results, roughly 30% of S&P 500 companies cited rising input costs − the highest share since 2022 − and eight of 11 sectors are expected to see declines in margins in 2Q. Although strong technology‑sector margins should keep overall profitability elevated relative to history, persistently high energy prices would raise downside risk going forward.”

In this environment, it’s only natural for investors to start gravitating toward dividend stocks. After all, if Adam is right, reliable income and defensive characteristics become more valuable in a volatile market. Dividend stocks offer investors a steady stream of cash flow while uncertainty surrounding margins, inflation, and energy costs continues to linger.

The analysts at Raymond James are on that case, picking out stocks that combine dividend yields of 7% or better and double-digit upside potential. But does the rest of Wall Street share that optimism? We turned to the TipRanks database to find out.

Delek Logistics Partners (DKL)

The first Raymond James pick that we’ll look at is Delek Logistics Partners, an energy asset company formed to own and operate the logistics, marketing, and midstream assets of the parent company, Delek US. DKL also acquires and develops additional assets in those same categories. The company’s total portfolio includes crude oil, refined products, and natural gas, as well as water used in the gathering processes. DKL gathers, processes, transports, and stores both crude oil and natural gas, and also markets, stores, and distributes a range of finished petroleum products.

DKL’s operations are located mainly in Texas, but also extend into Arkansas, Tennessee, and New Mexico. The company is a major operator in the Permian Basin, one of North America’s major hydrocarbon basins. Its infrastructure provides the essential background for safe, efficient, sustainable energy operations. The company’s produced water operations are key to the sustainable side of its overall business.

A few numbers show the scale of DKL’s business. The company has 9 distribution terminals for light products, approximately 1,188 miles of pipeline for crude oil and refined products, more than 200 MMcf/d of gas processing capacity, and well over 1.5 million Bbls/d of water disposal capacity.

Looking at the dividend, we find that DKL on April 23 declared a payment of $1.13 per unit – an increase from the previous payment of $1.125. At the new rate, the dividend annualizes to $4.52 per unit and gives a robust forward yield of 8.7%. The increased payout was paid out this past May 11.

In its 1Q26 financial release, DKL showed total revenues of $297.5 million – a total that was up 19% year-over-year and beat the forecast by over $57 million. The company had a GAAP EPS of $0.60 per share, missing the forecast by 20 cents per share. On a positive note, the company finished 1Q26 with over $9.9 million in cash and liquid assets, compared to $2.1 million at the end of the prior-year quarter.

Strong cash flow played a large part in Justin Jenkins’ outlook on the stock. The Raymond James expert wrote: “Despite a very volatile macro backdrop over the past few years, Delek Logistics has executed well to grow cash flow and is taking advantage of a solid footprint to capture incremental opportunities. Despite weather headwinds, 1Q26 was above our view, and we think the outlook remains solid, particularly in light of a much more constructive oil price environment. Despite solid growth and improved leverage, DKL trades at ~8x our 2028E EBITDA, at the bottom end of the midstream peer group (8-12x). DKL’s well-covered ~9% distribution yield is also near the highest in our coverage, adding to the total return opportunity. With what we see as an attractive entry point, we reiterate our Outperform rating and raise our target price to $60 on updated estimates.” (To watch Jenkins’ track record, click here.)

That’s the bullish view. Overall, DKL gets a Hold (i.e., Neutral) consensus rating from the Street’s analysts, based on 4 recent reviews that include 1 Buy and 3 Holds. The stock is trading for $51.70, and its $55.25 average target price implies a ~7% gain by this time next year. (See DKL stock forecast)
